**Acceptance Criteria**

No blocking findings. TSTA-01 is now covered by unit tests in [template.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/template.rs:1186), isolated temp-repo CLI integration tests in [apply_cli_tests.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/tests/apply_cli_tests.rs:213), and property tests in [template_apply_tests.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/tests/template_apply_tests.rs:904). The temp-repo tests use `TempDir` and write only under that repo’s `.jit` at [apply_cli_tests.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/tests/apply_cli_tests.rs:90).

TSTA-02 coverage is present for `--force` idempotence, update-in-place refresh, JSON apply-result output, explicit anchor binding, attached gate presets, acyclicity, transitive reduction, and pre-apply snapshot transform across [template_apply_tests.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/tests/template_apply_tests.rs:733), [template_apply_tests.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/tests/template_apply_tests.rs:912), [template_apply_tests.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/tests/template_apply_tests.rs:931), and [template_apply_tests.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/tests/template_apply_tests.rs:950).

**Prior Review Feedback**

The prior blockers have been addressed. New `#[cfg(test)]` unit tests were added in [template.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/template.rs:1186). A successful explicit `--anchor container=<epic>` CLI integration test now exists at [apply_cli_tests.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/tests/apply_cli_tests.rs:213).

**Architecture And Separation Of Concerns**

No boundary violations found. The production code change is test-only inside a `#[cfg(test)]` module. The integration tests exercise CLI behavior externally; command/domain/storage boundaries are not altered.

**Functional Paradigm**

No blocking issue. Added assertions mostly use iterator chains. The imperative reachability helper in [template_apply_tests.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/tests/template_apply_tests.rs:855) is test-only graph assertion code and is a reasonable pragmatic exception.

**Correctness And Invariants**

No production invariant regression found. The change does not modify file-write paths, event logging, gate transition semantics, label/assignee parsing, PID handling, or state mutation logic.

**Safety**

No unsafe code introduced in the changed Rust code.

**Testing**

`cargo-ci` is recorded as passed in the issue gate evidence; I did not rerun cargo tests per instruction. I ran `./scripts/jit-validate.sh`; it passed with label warnings only.

**Error Handling**

No new library fallible paths were introduced. `unwrap()`/`expect()` usage is confined to tests.

**CLI Contract**

No command contract regression found. The added explicit-anchor CLI test uses `--json` and verifies machine-readable fields and edge effects at [apply_cli_tests.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/tests/apply_cli_tests.rs:217).

**Documentation**

No new public APIs were added, so no new public doc-comment requirement applies.

**Jit Issue Management**

The prerequisite dependency `96fc8cd8` is marked `done` in the issue context. `./scripts/jit-validate.sh` passed; warnings are label warnings allowed by the review prompt.

VERDICT: PASS
